name: Wallace Roney
abstract: Wallace Roney (May 25, 1960 – March 31, 2020) was an American jazz (hard bop and post-bop) trumpeter. He won one Grammy award and was nominated twice.

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: the band consists of myself, Roy Haynes is playing drums, Wallace Roney is playing trumpet... So I’m really looking forward to playing with Chick this summer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: That record, I had gotten a grant from the National Endowment to put together a group and do some live concerts and stuff. That was the group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: But when I actually played the music with Wallace Roney... Doug has collaborated with... trumpeter Wallace Roney.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Javon Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Wallace Roney, who was a bandmate and good friend and someone I learned an immense amount from, he passed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Javon Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 3, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Lundy
about_quotes.evidence: Let’s pull up what you did and let’s see if we can take your plug-ins and somehow I’ll mix it with, you know, with Wallace Roney Junior or with Gifton Garrett — I mean Giveton Gelin — and let’s see what that sounds like just to have them both.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Lundy interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I think I remember telling Miles. I say, “I got this little guy from my hometown that plays trumpet, and his daddy is a boxer.” So that right there piqued his interest right away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
